task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'alpha_corp' workspace with the correct URI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a workspace named 'alpha_corp' in the GeoServer Workspaces list or edit page, with the Namespace URI set to 'http://www.alphacorp.com/data'.

task_completion.description: Verify the workspace is configured as isolated to hide it from global WFS capabilities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Isolated Workspace' checkbox being checked on the 'alpha_corp' workspace configuration page.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the PostGIS store named 'alpha_ds'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a data store named 'alpha_ds' in the Stores list, showing it is associated with the 'alpha_corp' workspace and is of type PostGIS.

task_completion.description: Verify the PostGIS store connection parameters are correct.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the 'alpha_ds' store configuration page, look for the connection parameters: host is 'gs-postgis', database is 'gis', and user is 'geoserver'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'ne_rivers' table is published as a layer named 'secure_rivers'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'secure_rivers' in the Layers list or the layer publishing page, showing it belongs to the 'alpha_corp' workspace and originates from the 'ne_rivers' resource.

task_completion.description: Verify the published layer has the correct title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the 'secure_rivers' layer configuration page (Data tab), look for the Title field containing exactly 'Alpha Corp Secure Hydrography'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GeoServer web interface to perform the configurations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoServer web administration GUI (http://localhost:8080/geoserver/web/) being actively used, rather than terminal windows executing cURL commands or REST API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the interaction is with the genuine GeoServer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface should display standard GeoServer menus, branding, and form layouts, not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass GeoServer to manipulate the database directly for layer creation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows showing direct psql commands or database administration tools being used to alter GeoServer's internal catalog.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected workspace name is 'alpha_corp'. The expected PostGIS store name is 'alpha_ds'. The expected layer name is 'secure_rivers' (published from the 'ne_rivers' table).
